in_frame: Tight crop of the upper portion of the Arduview console. The transparent clear-resin enclosure fills the frame. The display is active, showing a pixel-art 'Game Over / 13 Score / 13 High' screen in bright cyan-blue on a dark background. Below the display, through the clear shell, the PCB is visible: a large black IC chip, flex ribbon cable labeled UT-0205 / 94V-0, and board traces/components. Controls and lower body are out of frame. Background is a color-blocked flat-lay surface in olive/yellow-green, blue, and purple/lavender quadrants.

demonstrates.evidence: Display is clearly lit and legible — cyan pixel-art 'Game Over' text and score readout visible on the transparent pane; display module seated inside clear resin shell is fully readable
Through the clear resin shell below the display, a large black IC, flex ribbon cable, SMD components, and PCB traces are all plainly visible to the naked eye
Pixel-art bitmap font 'Game Over' screen with score tally visible on display confirms retro game running; no D-pad/buttons in frame
Clear resin enclosure edges are visible at frame borders; molded corners appear clean and even; detail-macro framing reveals no visible flash or parting-line defects in the resin
Color-blocked flat-lay background and close macro framing suggest deliberate product-object presentation, implying display/collectible intent, but no explicit display context
Flex ribbon cable with manufacturer marking (UT-0205 / 94V-0) and exposed PCB components visible through shell suggest open/hackable construction, but no Arduino or USB-C framing in shot

composition.background: Flat-lay color-blocked surface: olive/yellow-green top-left, blue top-right, purple/lavender bottom-left, sage/muted green bottom-right
composition.crop: Tight upper-body crop; lower third of device (controls, bottom edge) cut off; display and PCB internals centered

caption_hint: The screen is on. The board is visible. Nothing is hidden.
selection_note: Best single shot to simultaneously prove both load-bearing claims — on_legible display + PCB internals through shell — in one tight frame; use as anchor for transparency+gaming story.
